rationale_for_baseline_tier: The clinical picture strongly favours GORD (meal/recumbency trigger, acid regurgitation, partial antacid relief, no cardiac features), so this is not a T5 emergency like a positive-ACS constellation. But acute retrosternal chest pain cannot be cardiac-cleared via telehealth, so the safe baseline is prompt (24–48h, same-day if any doubt) in-person review with a low threshold for ECG (T2), start GORD treatment, and an explicit T5 cardiac safety-net. Under-triage weighting is respected by never closing this as confidently non-cardiac.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Chest tightness/pressure, pain radiating to the arm/jaw/neck, sweating, breathlessness, pain brought on by exertion, or severe/worsening or persistent chest pain
escalation_edges.rationale: These indicate possible ACS, which cannot be excluded remotely — emergency assessment with ECG/troponin is required.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get chest tightness or pressure, pain spreading to your arm, jaw or neck, sweating, breathlessness, or pain that comes on with exertion — call 000 immediately. Don't wait to see if antacids help.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Vomiting blood or coffee-ground material, black tarry stools, new difficulty swallowing, or unintentional weight loss
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests upper-GI bleeding, stricture or malignancy needing urgent assessment/endoscopy.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you vomit blood or dark coffee-ground material, pass black tarry stools, or develop trouble swallowing or weight loss — go to the Emergency Department / see a doctor urgently.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Cardiac risk factors (if present) would raise ACS probability
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: With significant cardiac risk factors, lower the threshold further toward same-day in-person ECG.
Emphasise calling 000 (not self-transport) for any cardiac features since no one else is present.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like reflux and a PPI plus not eating late and raising the head of the bed should help. Because chest pain can occasionally be cardiac and I can't check your heart remotely, arrange to be seen in person in the next day or so (sooner if any doubt) and consider an ECG. Call 000 immediately for any chest pressure, arm/jaw pain, sweating or breathlessness.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Start GORD treatment + prompt in-person review/ECG; 000 for cardiac features; ED for GI-bleed features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 straight away for any cardiac features at any time; do not wait.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Acute chest burning, clinically favouring GORD; ACS not excluded remotely — recommend ECG/in-person review; PPI + lifestyle started; GI alarm features negative.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Confidently reassuring 'just reflux', prescribing a PPI, and closing the encounter with no cardiac safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An atypical ACS is mislabelled as reflux; the patient is reassured, deteriorates overnight and arrests at home.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Treating GORD without acknowledging ACS cannot be excluded remotely or offering an ECG pathway.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling an ambulance for classic reflux with a fully negative cardiac screen and no risk factors — over-use of emergency resources (though understandable given chest pain).
